activity.body: Karen Chen, Discharge Planner at UCSF Mission Bay — booked intro for Thu 2:30 PM.

messages.body:
Hi Karen,

I'm Alex Girsh, I run Family Care SF — we're a family agency over on Ocean Ave, been doing this since 2001. Non-medical home care, HCO licensed.

The reason I'm writing: I know weekend discharges can be brutal when the family thought they could manage and then can't. If your usual list ever isn't picking up, give us a try. We'll get back to you with a real yes or no in a couple hours. And we don't say yes unless we can actually staff it — that part matters to us.

Not asking for a meeting or anything. Just wanted to be on your radar.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — that'd actually be useful. Weekend discharges fall through on us too often. Languages and overnight coverage matter. Thursday afternoon work for a quick call?

Thursday's good. We've got caregivers in English, Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish — overnights and weekends are kind of our thing. 2:30 work for you?

Alex
Confirmed — Thu May 14, 2:30 PM. Calendar invite sent.

activity.body: David Reyes, Elder Law at Reyes & Goldman — "send me your intake packet, I have two clients this week."

messages.body:
Hi David,

I'm Alex Girsh, I run Family Care SF over on Ocean. Family agency, HCO licensed, doing this since 2001 — non-medical home care.

Reason I'm reaching out: a fiduciary I work with mentioned attorneys often have a hard time finding home-care agencies that can do the boring stuff well — show up on time, leave clean notes, call you when something changes instead of when there's already a problem. That's mostly what we do.

If one of your clients ever needs care started, happy to be one of the names you call. I'll always tell you straight if we can or can't take it.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Send me your intake packet — I have two clients this week. One in Pacific Heights, one in Forest Hill. Both private pay, both need reliable starts.

activity.body: Priya Anand, Social Worker at Laguna Honda — asked about overnight rates and minimum hours.

messages.body:
Hi Priya,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, the family agency on Ocean Ave. Been doing non-medical home care since 2001, HCO licensed.

Quick note. We staff Russian and Tagalog speakers (and English/Spanish), which I know matters for some Laguna Honda discharges. If you ever have one going home and the language match is hard, give us a try — we'll get back to you fast and honestly on whether we can cover it.

Not trying to crash your list, just want you to know we exist.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Thanks Alex. Two quick questions: what's the overnight rate, and what's your minimum hours per shift? We have a Russian-speaking discharge tomorrow if you can staff it.

activity.body: Dr. Marcus Hall, Geriatrician at One Medical Pacific Heights — wants to add us to his referral list.

thread.subject: If you keep a home-care list

messages.body:
Hi Dr. Hall,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a small family agency on Ocean Ave. Non-medical home care, HCO licensed, doing this 24 years now.

I know you probably get these emails a lot, so I'll keep it short. If you keep a short list of home-care agencies you trust for patients who need help at home — companion, personal care, fall-risk support, overnights — I'd really appreciate being on it. We pick up the phone, we answer fast, and we won't say yes to a case unless we can actually staff it.

That's it. Thanks for reading.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Alex — yes, please add us to your one-pager list and I'll add you to mine. I refer about 3-5 a month. Do you cover Pacific Heights / Presidio Heights weekend overnights?

activity.body: Linda Okafor, Director at Rhoda Goldman Plaza — "not a fit right now, check back in Q3."

messages.body:
Hi Linda,

I'm Alex — I run Family Care SF, a family agency on Ocean Ave (HCO licensed, since 2001). We do non-medical private-duty: companion, personal care, fall-risk, overnights.

Not looking for a meeting. Just wanted to say hi and put us on your radar in case one of your residents starts needing more support than IL can give. Happy to be a backup name.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — appreciate the note. Not a fit right now; we have a preferred list locked in through Q2. Check back in Q3 and I'll take a real look.

activity.body: James Whitcombe, Admissions at Sequoias SF — 30-min call confirmed Mon 10:00 AM.

messages.body:
Hi James,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, the family agency on Ocean Ave. We've been doing this since 2001, HCO licensed, non-medical only.

The reason I'm writing — when an IL resident at a place like Sequoias starts to slip, the family usually doesn't realize it for another month or two, and by then it's a scramble. If your team ever wants a private-duty agency to step in early before things become a crisis, we're around. Companion, personal care, overnights, fall-risk. We answer fast and we'll always tell you up front if we can or can't staff something.

If 15 minutes makes sense at some point, I'd take it. No pressure.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — yes, worth a quick chat. Mon 10 AM PT works for me. 30 minutes. I'll send a Zoom link.

activity.body: Outreach batch sent — 52 contacts across hospitals, attorneys, and senior living.
Rachel Brodsky, Care Manager at Institute on Aging — open to a partnership conversation.

messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF. We're a family agency over on Ocean Ave, been at this since 2001. Non-medical home care, HCO licensed.

I know IOA already does a ton of care management, so I'm not pitching anything fancy. Just wanted to introduce ourselves as another option for when one of your plans needs reliable hands at home and your usual staffing partner is stretched. We're pretty straightforward — pick up the phone, answer fast, won't say yes unless we can actually do it.

If there's ever a case where it'd help, I'm just an email or call away.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Alex — open to a partnership conversation. We have plans that drift because the staffing partner isn't responsive. If you're consistent on communication and weekend coverage, we should talk.

lastWeek.recommendation: Prioritize the 3 warm leads above this week — same-week follow-up tends to convert ~2× better than waiting.
